sections.instruction: Complete the form below. Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS OR A NUMBER for each answer.

sections.instruction: According to the speaker, when did the following happen? Write the correct letter A, B or C next to questions 11-17.

sections.instruction: Complete the sentences below. Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS AND/ OR A NUMBER for each answer.

questions.content: Up to {{BLANK}} people attend garden parties at the palace each year.

sections.instruction: Answer the questions below. Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS for each answer.

questions.content: What aspect of history is it important to learn something from?
What do we also need to know about our ancestors?
Where are transferable skills useful?
What kind of approach to learning does social science use?

sections.instruction: What is said each of these subjects studied on a social studies course? Choose your answers from the list below and write the letters A-H next to questions 25-30.
sections.passage_or_context:
A This will focus on how each generation learns about its own culture
B This necessarily includes a study of physics and chemistry
C This is studied from the point of view of human behavior
D This will only be covered in terms of its theory
E This also covers the distribution of wealth
F This includes the study of archaeology
G This has received criticism for not being scientific enough
H This includes some work on urban planning

questions.content: Match each subject to the correct description.
options: A This will focus on how each generation learns about its own culture
B This necessarily includes a study of physics and chemistry
C This is studied from the point of view of human behavior
D This will only be covered in terms of its theory
E This also covers the distribution of wealth
F This includes the study of archaeology
G This has received criticism for not being scientific enough
H This includes some work on urban planning

sections.instruction: Choose the correct letter A, B or C.

questions.content: Students should complete their work on the 1950s
options: A if they want to be allowed to continue attending lectures
B because they will appreciate the information about the 1960s more
C otherwise they face the possibility of being failed for their coursework

questions.content: According to the lecturer, the ‘baby boom’ happened
options: A because of relaxed attitudes in the sixties
B during a time of war
C because people felt more secure

questions.content: In the sixties the USA had 70 million
According to the lecturer, compared to the 1950s, the 1960s were
According to the lecturer, literature changed the way women
options: A over 40 were treated by society
B viewed issues of race in society
C felt about their roles in society

questions.content: The rate of crime in the sixties
options: B was 9 times higher than in the fifties
C was 9 times lower than in the fifties

questions.content: What happened at the start of the 1960s?
options: B the introduction of the internet

sections.instruction: Complete the summary below. Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS for each answer.
sections.passage_or_context: In October, 1962, US President Kennedy met advisers to discuss {{BLANK}} which proved that the Cubans were installing nuclear missiles, presumably to use against the US. Kennedy was faced with three choices: to try to resolve the crisis diplomatically; to block the delivery of further weapons into Cuba; or to attack Cuba. Kennedy chose {{BLANK}} option, which prevented the build up of more missiles and led to the withdrawal of the existing ones. Most are agreed that a {{BLANK}} was narrowly avoided by Kennedy’s decision.

questions.content: In October, 1962, US President Kennedy met advisers to discuss {{BLANK}} which proved that the Cubans were installing nuclear missiles, presumably to use against the US.
Kennedy chose {{BLANK}} option, which prevented the build up of more missiles and led to the withdrawal of the existing ones.
Most are agreed that a {{BLANK}} was narrowly avoided by Kennedy’s decision.
